nodes.name: Contact Form Webhook
nodes.notes: Entry point. Receives contact form submissions as POST requests.

nodes.name: Validate Input
nodes.notes: Validates name and email exist and email format is correct. Throws early so bad data never reaches downstream systems.

nodes.name: Check Google Sheet
nodes.notes: Mocks a Google Sheets lookup. Checks if email already exists. Returns alreadyExists flag so the next node can branch.

nodes.name: Is New Contact?
nodes.notes: Branches on alreadyExists. True branch = new contact, add to sheet. False branch = skip sheet, go straight to email.

nodes.name: Add to Google Sheet
nodes.notes: Mocks appending a new row. In production swap for Google Sheets append node. Passes sheetRowId downstream for audit trail.

nodes.name: Skip Sheet (Already Exists)
nodes.notes: Explicit no-op node for the existing contact path. Makes the workflow readable — anyone looking at it knows this path was handled intentionally, not forgotten.

nodes.name: Merge Paths
nodes.notes: Reunites the two branches (new vs existing) so the email and Slack steps run for everyone.

nodes.name: Send Welcome Email
nodes.notes: Mocks email dispatch. Production version uses Gmail or SendGrid node. Email body is personalised with name and original message.

nodes.name: Post to Slack
nodes.notes: Mocks Slack notification. Message distinguishes new vs returning contacts so the team knows at a glance what happened.

nodes.name: Success Response
nodes.notes: Builds a clean summary response. Useful for debugging and for any upstream caller that wants confirmation.

nodes.name: Error Handler
nodes.notes: Catches any failure in the workflow. Logs which node failed and why. In production this would also alert the team via Slack or email so nothing silently breaks.
